results.name: alloc::raw_vec::handle_reserve_error
results.rationale: Function only uses rdi (8 bytes scalar); test rdi, rdi splits CapacityOverflow (zero) from AllocError (NonZeroUsize) — niche-optimized TryReserveErrorKind fits exactly in rdi.

results.name: _ZN5alloc7raw_vec20RawVecInner$LT$A$GT$7reserve21do_reserve_and_handle17hc03c74bc2b9610b6E
results.transport_error:
sdk: Exception: Command failed with exit code 1 (exit code: 1)
Error output: Check stderr output for details
results.rationale: Standard RawVecInner::do_reserve_and_handle wrapper; rdi=&mut RawVecInner{ptr,cap}, rsi=len, rdx=additional forwarded to grow_amortized.

results.name: _ZN5alloc3vec16Vec$LT$T$C$A$GT$7reserve17h1ca9604a9d10a27bE
results.rationale: Vec::reserve(&mut self, additional: usize) - rdi reads &mut Vec<T> fields (ptr at +0x00, len at +0x10), rsi is the additional count, no return.

results.name: _ZN5alloc7raw_vec19RawVec$LT$T$C$A$GT$8grow_one17h18b350cb40a12ebdE
results.rationale: Receiver is &mut self of a 16-byte (ptr,cap) wrapper; T=u8 from grow_amortized(_, _, 1, 1, 1) layout(size=1,align=1). Box<[u8]> is the idiomatic 16-byte ptr+len wrapper the harness suggested.

results.name: _ZN5alloc7raw_vec20RawVecInner$LT$A$GT$10deallocate17h75b25f702d0b934cE
results.rationale: 16-byte receiver struct (ptr, len) at offsets 0,8 matches Box<[u8]>; rsi+rdx form a Layout pair; body tail-calls free(ptr, cap*size, align).

results.name: _ZN5alloc7raw_vec20RawVecInner$LT$A$GT$15try_allocate_in17hfa9b309c0053cc89E
results.submitted_signature: (capacity: usize, init: AllocInit, alloc: Global, elem_layout: Layout) -> Result<Box<[u8]>, TryReserveError>
results.rationale: RawVecInner::try_allocate_in: rdi=sret 24B Result, rsi=capacity, rdx=AllocInit (read via mov ecx,edx; test cl,cl - dispatches __rdl_alloc vs __rdl_alloc_zeroed), Global ZST, rcx/r8=Layout(align,size); Ok payload=(ptr,len)=Box<[u8]> matches the 16B output region; Err=TryReserveError with CapacityOverflow/AllocError{Layout} variants observed in HLIL writes.

results.name: _ZN70_$LT$alloc..vec..Vec$LT$T$C$A$GT$$u20$as$u20$core..ops..drop..Drop$GT$4drop17hb8c2272cd6624092E
results.rationale: Symbol identifies this as Vec<T,A>::drop; function body is a 6-byte no-op stub (likely COMDAT-folded), so T narrows by caller context.

results.name: _ZN77_$LT$alloc..raw_vec..RawVec$LT$T$C$A$GT$$u20$as$u20$core..ops..drop..Drop$GT$4drop17h077ee2decf6fd162E
results.rationale: Function tail-jumps to dealloc with rdi=ptr@+8, rsi=size@+0, rdx=align=1; T has size_of=1 and align=1 (i.e. u8); the receiver is a 16B fat-pointer wrapper that idiomatic Rust spells as Box<[u8]>.

results.name: _ZN5alloc3fmt6format12format_inner17h8340a7f817b89373E
results.rationale: Standard alloc::fmt::format::format_inner takes Arguments<'_> and returns String; sret on rdi (16+8 writes) and rsi pointing at a 48B Arguments laid out as three slice headers matches exactly.

results.name: clone_bytes
results.rationale: Reads ptr at rsi+8 and len at rsi+0x10, allocates a buffer of size=len align=1, memcpy's len bytes into it, then writes (cap=len, ptr, len) into the 24-byte sret slot rdi - canonical Vec<u8>::clone returning by sret.
